title: Quiz: Hedging Strategies
description: Test your understanding of derivative hedging techniques

questions.question: What does delta hedging achieve?

options.text: Neutralizes sensitivity to small price changes

questions.explanation: Delta hedging neutralizes the portfolio's first-order sensitivity to underlying price changes. A delta-neutral portfolio has approximately zero P&L for small price moves.

questions.question: To delta hedge a long call option, what position is needed?
questions.explanation: A long call has positive delta. To create a delta-neutral portfolio, you need the opposite exposure: short shares of the underlying. The number of shares equals the option's delta.

questions.question: Why is gamma hedging beneficial?

options.text: It reduces the need for frequent delta rebalancing

questions.explanation: Gamma hedging makes delta more stable. With lower gamma, delta changes less when the underlying moves, reducing how often the delta hedge needs to be adjusted.

questions.question: What causes hedging errors in discrete delta hedging?

options.text: Price moves between rebalancing plus gamma exposure

questions.explanation: Discrete hedging errors occur because the underlying moves between rebalances. Due to gamma, delta changes with price, making the hedge stale. Larger moves cause larger errors.

questions.question: Vega hedging protects against changes in implied volatility.
questions.explanation: True. Vega measures sensitivity to volatility changes. Vega hedging neutralizes this exposure, protecting the portfolio from gains or losses due to volatility movements.

questions.question: Why can't you gamma hedge with the underlying stock?
questions.explanation: The underlying stock has a linear payoff with respect to itself, meaning its delta is always 1 and its gamma is 0. To hedge gamma, you need instruments with non-zero gamma, i.e., options.

questions.question: What is the main cost of frequent delta hedging?
questions.explanation: Frequent rebalancing incurs transaction costs (commissions, bid-ask spreads, market impact). More frequent hedging reduces hedging error but increases transaction costs.

questions.question: Which Greeks can be hedged using other options? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Gamma and vega can only be hedged using other options since the underlying has zero gamma and zero vega. Delta can be hedged with the underlying, and theta is typically a cost of holding options rather than something actively hedged.
